title: 503 Service Unavailable from custom node
symptom: When executing a custom node that makes API requests, a 503 Service Unavailable error is returned. This occurs specifically when targeting a certain external API.
applies_when: Using a custom node with HTTP requests
not_this_if: Error occurs with other APIs like google.de
Using a different HTTP request method
root_cause: The 503 error is caused by the target API's web application firewall blocking requests from n8n. This can happen if the API provider has security measures that restrict certain user agents or request patterns.
fix_steps: Modify the custom node to set a specific User-Agent header
Use a value other than 'n8n' for the User-Agent
Test the API request again to ensure it succeeds
agent_instruction: Set a custom User-Agent in your HTTP request to bypass the API's firewall restrictions.
